credit_cycle.trend: Banken verschärfen Kreditstandards moderat. Q4 2025: Verschärfung bei Gewerbekrediten, aber Lockerung bei Autokrediten
credit_cycle.evidence: Fed SLOOS zeigt gemischte Signale. Kreditnachfrage schwächt sich ab, Standards werden selektiv verschärft

fiscal_impulse.evidence: Q1 FY2026: 1.8 Billionen USD Ausgaben, 602 Mrd. USD Defizit. Zolleinnahmen 90 Mrd. USD (4x Vorjahr). Brookings FIM erwartet 2 Prozentpunkte BIP-Wachstum Q1 2026

dollar_liquidity.dxy_trend: DXY bei 99.28 (24. März), erholt von Februar-Tief 96, aber unter März-Peak 101. Iran-Konflikt stützt Dollar kurzfristig
dollar_liquidity.evidence: Geopolitische Spannungen und Fed-Pause bei 3.75% stützen Dollar. Struktureller Abwärtstrend intakt nach Konfliktende erwartet

current_narrative.title: Stagflationary Energy Shock
current_narrative.description: Der Markt ist von einem klassischen stagflationären Schock erfasst: Der Iran-Krieg und die Blockade der Straße von Hormuz haben Ölpreise auf über 100 Dollar getrieben, während gleichzeitig das Wirtschaftswachstum schwächelt. Die Fed ist in einer Policy-Falle gefangen zwischen steigender Inflation und schwächelnder Konjunktur. Gleichzeitig verstärkt die AI-Disruption-Angst die Rotation aus Software-Aktien.

narrative_shift.trigger_event: Iran-Krieg und Blockade der Straße von Hormuz ab 28. Februar 2026

supporting_evidence.fact: Brent Crude stieg von unter 70 Dollar auf über 112 Dollar (70% Anstieg)
Fed hielt Zinsen bei 3,5-3,75% und projiziert nur noch einen Zinsschnitt 2026
Märkte preisen jetzt 50% Wahrscheinlichkeit für Zinserhöhung statt Zinssenkungen
Eurozone PMI fiel auf 50,5 (10-Monats-Tief) bei steigenden Inputkosten
US Q4 GDP wurde auf nur 0,7% annualisiert nach unten revidiert
Producer Price Index stieg um 0,7% monatlich (doppelt so stark wie erwartet)

contradicting_evidence.fact: S&P 500 ist trotz aller Schocks nur 3% gefallen
contradicting_evidence.why_important: Zeigt bemerkenswerte Markt-Resilienz trotz stagflationärer Signale

contradicting_evidence.fact: Trump kündigte 5-tägigen Stopp der Iran-Angriffe an nach produktiven Gesprächen
contradicting_evidence.why_important: Könnte zu schneller Deeskalation und Ölpreis-Rückgang führen

contradicting_evidence.fact: Korrelation zwischen S&P 500 Aktien ist auf 13% gefallen (niedrigster Wert seit 2022)
contradicting_evidence.why_important: Niedrige Korrelation isoliert den Index vor breiten Sell-offs
Große Bank sieht noch keine Rezession trotz stagflationärer Signale

contradicting_evidence.fact: Gold fiel 10% in der schlechtesten Woche seit 2011
contradicting_evidence.why_important: Traditioneller Inflationsschutz versagt, deutet auf Liquiditätsdruck statt echte Stagflation

market_positioning.crowded_trade: Long Energy (XOM, CVX), Short Software (CRM, PLTR), Long Real Assets
market_positioning.contrarian_opportunity: Long Quality Software nach AI-Disruption-Überreaktion, Long Bonds bei extremen Yields

narrative.narrative_risk: Falls der Iran-Konflikt schnell beigelegt wird, kollabiert das Stagflations-Narrativ binnen Tagen. Ölpreise würden um 30-40% fallen, die Fed könnte wieder dovish werden, und die massive Rotation aus Software-Aktien würde sich umkehren. Energy-Aktien, Commodities und Inflationsschutz-Trades würden am stärksten fallen, während Growth-Tech und Duration am stärksten steigen würden.

cftc_highlights.net_position: Spekulanten sind netto long mit 215.445 Kontrakten long vs. 52.313 short (Stand 10. März). Managed Money hält 52% der Open Interest.
cftc_highlights.contrarian_signal: Extrem bullische Positionierung bei Gold deutet auf überfüllten Trade hin. Contrarian-Signal für mögliche Korrektur.

cftc_highlights.net_position: Non-Commercial Trader sind netto long mit 33.306 long vs. 8.728 short. Starke spekulative Positionierung.
cftc_highlights.contrarian_signal: Sehr bullische Silber-Positionierung. Crowded Trade mit Korrekturrisiko.

cftc_highlights.net_position: Spekulanten sind netto long mit 82.373 long vs. 30.707 short. Moderate Positionierung.
cftc_highlights.contrarian_signal: Kupfer-Positionierung weniger extrem, aber immer noch bullisch geneigt.

fund_flows.flow: Gemischte Flows: SPY verzeichnete 9.01 Mrd. USD Zuflüsse in der Woche bis 13. März
Abflüsse von 1.63 Mrd. USD in der Woche bis 11. März, Verbesserung gegenüber -2.59 Mrd. USD Vorwoche
Europäische Gold-ETFs verzeichneten Abflüsse von 582.53 Mio. EUR in der Woche bis 20. März

leverage.margin_debt_level: Rekordniveau von 1.28 Billionen USD erreicht (Februar 2026), 36% Anstieg gegenüber Vorjahr
leverage.risk_assessment: Extrem hohe Verschuldung bei 4.1% des BIP vs. historischer Median von 1.5%. Systemisches Risiko bei Marktkorrektur.

slow_burns.current_status: BDC NAV discounts at 17% (Cliffwater index), matching June 2022 crisis levels. Redemptions hit 5% quarterly cap across major non-traded funds. OBDC trading at 25% discount to stated NAV. Software exposure creating systematic overvaluation concerns.
slow_burns.tipping_point: Redemption requests exceed 20% quarterly for two consecutive quarters, forcing asset fire sales
slow_burns.time_to_tipping: Q2-Q3 2026 based on BREIT parallel patterns

slow_burns.current_status: BOJ raised rates to 0.75% in March 2026. USD/JPY at 156-157 range with intervention threats at 160. JGB 10-year yields hit 25-year high at 1.95%. Carry trade positions showing controlled unwinding rather than collapse.
slow_burns.tipping_point: USD/JPY breaks below 145 or BOJ signals rates toward 1.5-2.0%

slow_burns.current_status: LGFV debt estimated at 60 trillion RMB (48% of GDP). Only 19% can service interest without government subsidies. Property sales collapse continues into fifth year with 80 million vacant homes. Local governments implementing asset liquidation task forces.
slow_burns.tipping_point: 5% LGFV default rate would increase banking system NPLs by 75%
slow_burns.time_to_tipping: Late 2026 as land collateral values continue falling

slow_burns.topic: US Commercial Real Estate Office Collapse
slow_burns.current_status: Office vacancy hit 20.6% in Q2 2025, continuing six-quarter deterioration. 936 billion in CRE mortgages mature in 2026. Office delinquency rates at 12% with values down 30% since 2022 peak. Regional banks heavily exposed.
slow_burns.tipping_point: Office vacancy exceeds 25% with refinancing rates 200bp above maturing loans

observations.status: BDC NAV discounts at 17% (Cliffwater index), matching June 2022 crisis levels. Redemptions hit 5% quarterly cap across major non-traded funds. OBDC trading at 25% discount to stated NAV. Software exposure creating systematic overvaluation concerns.

BOJ raised rates to 0.75% in March 2026. USD/JPY at 156-157 range with intervention threats at 160. JGB 10-year yields hit 25-year high at 1.95%. Carry trade positions showing controlled unwinding rather than collapse.

LGFV debt estimated at 60 trillion RMB (48% of GDP). Only 19% can service interest without government subsidies. Property sales collapse continues into fifth year with 80 million vacant homes. Local governments implementing asset liquidation task forces.

Office vacancy hit 20.6% in Q2 2025, continuing six-quarter deterioration. 936 billion in CRE mortgages mature in 2026. Office delinquency rates at 12% with values down 30% since 2022 peak. Regional banks heavily exposed.

feedback_loops.loop_description: Yen strengthening forces carry trade unwind, triggering VIX spike and equity selling, which drives more safe-haven flows to yen, accelerating the cycle
feedback_loops.current_stage: Early stage - controlled unwinding with VIX at 17 vs normal 14
feedback_loops.break_condition: BOJ verbal intervention or Fed emergency rate cuts to restore carry differentials

feedback_loops.loop_description: Private credit redemptions force NAV markdowns, widening discount to public BDCs, triggering more redemptions as investors lose confidence in private marks
feedback_loops.current_stage: Mid-stage - hitting quarterly caps but not yet forcing fire sales
feedback_loops.break_condition: Sponsor capital injections or redemption gate extensions beyond quarterly limits

feedback_loops.loop_description: China property weakness reduces LGFV land revenues, forcing more borrowing, which increases debt service costs and reduces creditworthiness, limiting future borrowing capacity
feedback_loops.current_stage: Advanced stage - asset liquidation task forces already formed
feedback_loops.break_condition: Central government direct bailout or property market stabilization

observations.stage: Early stage - controlled unwinding with VIX at 17 vs normal 14
Mid-stage - hitting quarterly caps but not yet forcing fire sales
Advanced stage - asset liquidation task forces already formed

silence_signals.why_notable: Historical pattern shows intervention at these levels, but none occurred during recent spike to 157.89
silence_signals.implication: Either intervention threshold raised or BOJ confidence in rate normalization path

silence_signals.expected_event: Fed mention of financial stability concerns in March FOMC minutes
silence_signals.why_notable: Private credit stress and CRE issues typically trigger Fed commentary
silence_signals.implication: Fed may be underestimating systemic risks or maintaining strategic silence

silence_signals.expected_event: China announcing new property stimulus despite weak data
silence_signals.why_notable: Typical response pattern to continued property sector deterioration
silence_signals.implication: Structural shift away from property-led growth model may be permanent

major_releases_missed.summary: Global growth projected 3.3% for 2026, slight upward revision. Technology investment offsetting trade headwinds. Key risks include geopolitical tensions and technology expectation reevaluation.
major_releases_missed.portfolio_relevance: Confirms our thesis on AI investment supporting markets despite macro headwinds

major_releases_missed.summary: EMDE growth projections mixed with downside risks from trade tensions. Sub-Saharan Africa growth firming to 4.3%. China growth declining to 4.4% in 2026.
major_releases_missed.portfolio_relevance: Supports underweight EM positioning, particularly China exposure

correlation_breaks.normal_correlation: Typically negative correlation as yen strengthens during risk-off periods
correlation_breaks.current_behavior: Correlation tightening significantly in 2026 - yen moves immediately drive VIX spikes
correlation_breaks.what_followed: VIX hit 65, S&P fell 8% in three days before Fed intervention expectations stabilized markets

correlation_breaks.pair: Private Credit NAV vs Public BDC Prices
correlation_breaks.normal_correlation: Private marks typically lag public prices by 1-2 quarters
correlation_breaks.current_behavior: 17% discount in public BDCs while private funds maintain stable NAVs
correlation_breaks.what_followed: Forced markdowns, redemption gates, and eventual convergence at distressed levels

correlation_breaks.pair: China Property vs LGFV Credit Spreads
correlation_breaks.normal_correlation: Property weakness typically leads LGFV stress by 6-12 months
correlation_breaks.current_behavior: Both deteriorating simultaneously with no lag
correlation_breaks.last_time_this_happened: Never at this scale - new structural relationship
correlation_breaks.what_followed: Unknown - suggests deeper systemic linkage than previously understood

biases.description: Systeme sind US-lastig. Japan Carry Trade, China Property, Europe Sovereign Stress nur im Weekly Unpriced Risk Scan.
Cycles Circle nimmt an dass Zyklen sich wiederholen. Strukturbrüche können historische Muster ungültig machen.
